titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 620

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=814 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.68676+00 | translation moa=815 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.697066+00

div.p:
Alexander dei gratia Rex Scottorum . Omnibus probis Hominibus tocius terre sue Salutem . Sciatis nos monasterium de
Coldingham’ . Priorem et Conuentum eiusdem Monasterii . Homines et terras suas . ac vniuersas eorundem possessiones .
sub firma pace et protectione nostra iuste suscepisse . Quare firmiter prohibemus ne quis eis malum . iniu
riam . molestiam aut grauamen aliquod iniuste presumat inferre  Super nostram plenariam forisfacturam .
Testibus . Magistro Ricardo de Inuirkethyn’ . Camerario . Roberto de Meyners’ . Willelmo de Haya . et Galfrido de Boscho . apud
Tynigham’ . viij . die Aprilis . Anno regni domini Regis  xxx Quinto .

p.cl: Alexander by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of the whole of his land greeting.

Know us in accordance with law to have received the monastery of Coldingham, the prior and convent of the same monastery, their men and lands, and all the possessions of the same, under our firm peace and protection. Wherefore do we strictly forbid that anyone should venture to bring about any evil, injury, annoyance or grievance to them, on pain of our full forfeiture.
As witnesses, Master Richard of Inverkeithing, chamberlain, Robert of Menzies, William de la Haye, and Geoffrey del Bois,
at Tynninghame, the 8th day of April, in the year of the reign of the lord king, the thirty-fifth [1249].
